title: Ricinocarpus ocymoides
taxonomy: Ricinocarpus ocymoides is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationships with other species. The genus Ricinocarpus is a small group of plants that are native to Australia. uses: Ricinocarpus ocymoides, although information on this specific species is scarce, is likely to be used in traditional medicine by various indigenous cultures. side_effects: Ricinocarpus ocymoides, also known as the Australian saltbush, is a plant that may have some potential side effects when consumed. There is limited information available on the specific side effects of Ricinocarpus ocymoides, but it is generally recommended to exercise caution when using any new or unproven remedies.
